body:
STATUS 2026-07-22. Items (2) and (3) LANDED (PR 155, corrected by PR 170). Item (a) RESOLVED as a
convention (PR 171). Items (b), (c), (d) remain, and a fresh conformance pass added more — this
ticket is now the register of everything ADLC ships that Loam does not yet use.

LANDED — the ledgers exist: findings.jsonl and manifest.jsonl (hash-chained, verify exits 0).

CORRECTED — the .gitignore recommendation in the previous body was taken and then REVERSED, and the
reversal is the durable fact. Deny-scratch-only deviates from ADLC's own standard, which is an
ALLOWLIST shipped in adlc init: commit config.json / tickets.json / tickets/ / specs/ (the
CONTRACT); keep findings.jsonl, manifest.jsonl, ticket-transactions/ LOCAL — per-worktree evidence
that would collide across branches. The reviewer's concern (a new evidence file is silently
untracked under an allowlist) is answered by re-running adlc init, which adds the negation when a
file joins the contract. None of that was discoverable before, because init had NEVER RUN here: on
Windows it dies EINVAL opening any file that already exists (O_TRUNC without O_CREAT) after happily
creating the ones that did not. Fixed by scripts/patch-adlc-init.mjs (win32-only O_CREAT); npm run
adlc:patch applies it alongside the npx patch.

RESOLVED (a) — RAILS ON THE REST OF THE BACKLOG: do NOT backfill, now for two independent reasons.
The original stands — a rail written by the author of already-merged code shares that code's
premise, which is how three hollow rails shipped on 2026-07-21. The second was probed 2026-07-22: a
rail glob matching NO FILE does not fail and does not warn; rails-guard prints "all checks passed"
and exits 0. Pre-declaring rail paths on todo tickets therefore protects nothing while manufacturing
a green. Rails are a P3 artifact, earned when the tests exist. CLAUDE.md P3 now says so.

REMAINING FROM THE ORIGINAL BODY:

(b) A rail-authored-on-branch SUPPRESSION for rails-guard, so the honest first-branch case passes
cleanly and ADLC_RAILS_BYPASS stays rare enough to be worth scrutinising. Confirmed live on the T62
branch: rails-guard exits 2 against the freeze commit for an unused-import cleanup with the
assertions untouched. A TRUE positive, and the gate's first demonstrated catch here — but exactly
the benign case that wants a suppression rather than a bypass.

(c) BYPASS ATTESTATION. A rails-bypass manifest entry carries a free-text why written by the
bypassing party. It should carry the finding ids or prosecutor run that justify it, hash-linked.

(d) The backfilled H1-H7 ledger entries were filed against hand-picked files, and at least two do
not exhibit the hazard they record (H7 against store/sqlite.ts, whose real sites are publish and
promote/adopt.ts; H3 and H5 both against lifecycle.ts as a stand-in). P7 distill clusters by
recurrence, so a hazard filed at line 1 of an innocent module either fails to cluster or
manufactures a false one. Re-point them, or flag them structurally synthetic. STAKES CHANGED:
findings.jsonl is now LOCAL and per-worktree, so a mis-pointed entry corrupts only this clone's P7
clustering — the durable home for a hazard is SUBSTRATE-HAZARDS.md.

FOUND 2026-07-22, ADLC machinery still unused (each is small on its own; together they are the
difference between running ADLC and describing it):

(e) NO rails-guard IN CI. ADLC calls this "the unbypassable commit-time backstop" and ships a
template at docs/ci/rails-guard.yml to be wired as a REQUIRED check. The plugin's PreToolUse hook is
only the in-session layer, and ADLC is explicit that Bash is NOT gated in-session — a shell cannot
be reliably parsed, so rail mutations via Bash are caught ONLY by the CI diff gate. We have no such
check, so today the in-session hook is the whole defense and any Bash-mediated rail edit is
unguarded. Highest-value item here.

(f) LEGACY TICKET STORE. ADLC P0 says new repos use one canonical shard per ticket under
.adlc/tickets/, with .adlc/tickets.json "supported until an approved migration". We are on the
legacy single file, which is also why every ticket PR collides in the same file (three conflicts in
one day). adlc ticket store migrate exists. Worth doing deliberately, not incidentally.

(g) adlc run <phase> HAS NEVER BEEN INVOKED — exit 2 on all seven phases. It asserts phase evidence
by EXACT name, and every gate name we recorded (prosecute, rails-guard, ticket-update, rails-bypass)
matches nothing it asserts. The canonical names are now in CLAUDE.md; recording and asserting them
per ticket is the follow-through.

(h) NEVER-RUN TOOLS: behavior-diff (capture/compare — ADLC's intended way to make change visible for
the P6 human gate, instead of prose), review-calibration (scores whether review catches injected
mutants — who reviews the reviewer), adlc accept (the P6 acceptance packet), and P7's three gates
(lesson-foundry, rejection-mining, skill-rot).

(i) spec-lint --llm / --prompt-only catches VACUOUS verification methods; plain spec-lint only
catches MISSING ones. A criterion naming a test that does not test it passes today.

(j) OPEN QUESTION FOR MYK — CROSS-MODEL REVIEW. ADLC gates P5 on adversarial-review with >=2
DISTINCT providers (cross-model, fresh context) and states that for the trust-root tier a clean
SAME-model P5 is NOT enough: the prosecute runner exits 2 until the manifest holds a
cross-model-review approve from a provider distinct from the author. Its trust-root list names
.adlc/tickets.json — which nearly every one of our PRs touches. AMBIGUITY: the other paths in that
list (packages/rails-guard, scripts/rails-guard-ci.mjs, docs/ci/rails-guard.yml) are ADLC's OWN repo
files, so the rule may be ADLC dogfooding its own governance rather than a consumer-repo
requirement. Our P5 has been a same-model subagent panel throughout. Needs a call: does Loam wire a
second provider, and if so where does the key come from?
title: Turn on the ADLC machinery we have never used: rails, findings ledger, gate evidence
